item.title: Daily Briefing - 2026-05-24
item.description:
**Morning Briefing**
**1) What changed since yesterday** Global markets opened slightly lower. Key U.S. indices are down about half a percent. Inflation data is still weighing on sentiment. Oil prices have risen due to OPEC signals of sustained cuts.
**2) Venezuela update** Venezuela's parliament has approved a new electoral process. This could lead to presidential elections early next year. International observers are expected to monitor these elections closely.
**3) UK response signals** The UK government has announced new sanctions on Russian individuals. This follows the latest developments regarding Ukraine. The aim is to apply further pressure on the Russian economy.
**4) Legal/docket watch (Epstein-related)** None.
**5) What to watch today** Watch for U.S. consumer sentiment data, scheduled for release this afternoon. Market reactions may indicate investor confidence levels. Also, observe any developments in the Venezuela elections. Lastly, keep an eye on OPEC's discussions at their meeting today.
End of briefing.
